skill.name: parallel-agents
skill.description: Multi-agent orchestration patterns for Claude Code. Coordinate multiple specialized agents to tackle complex tasks requiring diverse expertise.
skill.summary: Orchestrate multiple specialized agents through Claude Code's native agent system for comprehensive analysis and multi-domain tasks.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: This skill is documentation-only content teaching users how to orchestrate Claude Code's built-in agents. Static analyzer flagged 32 false positives for markdown code block delimiters (```) misidentified as Ruby shell backticks, and 3 false positives for 'weak cryptography' in YAML descriptions and table cells. No executable code exists in this skill. All findings dismissed as false positives.

content.user_title: Orchestrate Multiple Specialized Agents in Parallel
content.value_statement: Complex tasks require diverse expertise that single agents cannot provide. This skill enables coordinated multi-agent workflows through Claude Code's native agent system for comprehensive analysis.
actual_capabilities: Coordinate multiple specialized agents with different domain expertise in a single session
Execute sequential agent chains with context passing between agents
Resume and continue previous agent work with stored context
Synthesize findings from multiple agents into unified reports
Access 17 specialized agents including security, backend, frontend, and testing experts
Leverage Claude Code built-in Explore and Plan agents alongside custom agents
limitations: Requires Claude Code environment with agent support enabled
Not suitable for simple single-domain tasks that one agent can handle
All agents share the same session context which may have token limits
Orchestration overhead may be excessive for quick fixes or small changes

use_cases.title: Comprehensive Code Review
use_cases.description: Run security-auditor, backend-specialist, frontend-specialist, and test-engineer agents in sequence to review a pull request from multiple perspectives, then synthesize all findings into a single actionable report.

use_cases.title: Feature Implementation
use_cases.description: Orchestrate database-architect for schema design, backend-specialist for API implementation, frontend-specialist for UI components, and test-engineer for coverage verification in a coordinated workflow.

use_cases.title: Security Audit Pipeline
use_cases.description: Execute security-auditor for configuration review, penetration-tester for vulnerability assessment, and synthesize prioritized remediation steps for development teams.

prompt_templates.title: Beginner: Single Agent Task
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the security-auditor agent to review the authentication module for common vulnerabilities and report findings.
prompt_templates.scenario: Quick security check on a specific component

prompt_templates.title: Intermediate: Sequential Chain
prompt_templates.prompt: First, use the explorer-agent to map the project structure. Then, have the backend-specialist review all API endpoints. Finally, ask the test-engineer to identify test coverage gaps based on those findings.
prompt_templates.scenario: Multi-step codebase analysis with context passing

prompt_templates.title: Advanced: Parallel Analysis with Synthesis
prompt_templates.prompt: Run the security-auditor, performance-optimizer, and test-engineer agents in parallel on the checkout module. Synthesize all findings into a prioritized action plan with critical issues first.

prompt_templates.title: Expert: Resume and Extend
prompt_templates.prompt: Resume the previous agent session and continue with additional requirements: add mobile compatibility review using the mobile-developer agent and update the synthesis report accordingly.

output_examples.input: Run a comprehensive review of the user authentication system
output: Security-auditor identified missing rate limiting on login endpoint
Backend-specialist flagged inconsistent error handling across auth routes
Test-engineer found 40% coverage gap in password reset flow
Synthesis: Critical rate limiting fix needed, error handling standardization recommended, additional test coverage required

output_examples.input: Analyze the React component library for accessibility issues
output: Frontend-specialist identified 12 components missing ARIA labels
Explorer-agent mapped 47 interactive components across 8 directories
Recommendations: Add ARIA labels to form inputs, implement keyboard navigation for dropdowns, add screen reader announcements for dynamic content

best_practices: Follow logical agent order: discovery first, then analysis, implementation, and testing last
Pass relevant context between agents to avoid redundant analysis and maintain coherence
Always include a synthesis step to consolidate findings into actionable recommendations
anti_patterns: Using multiple agents for simple single-domain tasks that one agent can handle efficiently
Running agents in parallel without planning how to synthesize their potentially conflicting outputs
Skipping the test-engineer agent when implementing code modifications

faq.question: What is the difference between built-in agents and custom agents?
faq.answer: Built-in agents (Explore, Plan, General-purpose) are native to Claude Code with fixed capabilities. Custom agents like security-auditor or backend-specialist provide domain-specific expertise and can be orchestrated together for complex workflows.

faq.question: Can I run multiple agents simultaneously?
faq.answer: Yes, you can invoke multiple agents in parallel for independent tasks. However, for sequential workflows where later agents need earlier findings, use a chain pattern with explicit context passing.

faq.question: How do I resume an interrupted agent session?
faq.answer: Use the resume command with the agent ID to continue from where the previous session ended. All context from the prior session is preserved and available for continuation.

faq.question: When should I use orchestration instead of a single agent?
faq.answer: Use orchestration for complex tasks requiring multiple expertise domains, comprehensive reviews from different perspectives, or feature implementations spanning backend, frontend, and database work.

faq.question: What happens if agents produce conflicting recommendations?
faq.answer: The synthesis step consolidates all findings and resolves conflicts by prioritizing based on severity. Security issues typically take precedence, followed by functional correctness, then performance optimizations.

faq.question: Is there a limit to how many agents I can orchestrate?
faq.answer: There is no hard limit on agent count, but practical limits exist due to session token constraints. For large orchestrations, consider grouping related agents and synthesizing in stages.
